title: Robinhood Q4 2025 Earnings Analysis: Revenue Miss, EPS Beat
text: Robinhood Markets reported mixed fourth-quarter results. The company exceeded earnings expectations with earnings per share of $0.66 vs. analysts' consensus estimate of $0.63, while falling short on the top line. Revenue Breakdown: Total Q4 sales: $1.28 billion (vs. $1.35 billion expected). Transaction-based revenue: $776 million (vs. $797.6 million anticipated). Market Impact: Despite beating on earnings, investor sentiment turned negative. Shares declined 5.4% immediately following the earnings release, suggesting the revenue shortfall overshadowed the earnings outperformance. Performance Context: The stock showed significant gains in prior periods—up 193% in 2024 and 204% in 2025—but recent volatility in cryptocurrency markets has pressured the share price. The trading platform's exposure to crypto fluctuations appears to be a key headwind affecting investor confidence despite solid profitability metrics. Full Year 2025: Record revenues of $4.5 billion, record diluted EPS of $2.05, and record net deposits of $68 billion.
